name: Come Sit With Us - A Meetup by My Divorce Guide
description:
It is an intimate, offline gathering designed for those navigating separation, relationship transitions, or quiet personal reinvention. It is a gentle, held space where stories will be shared across the room — without performance, without spotlight, without the pressure to “have it all figured out.”
Some stories will be read aloud. Some will simply be witnessed.
You are welcome to speak, or just sit and listen.
Why this room?
Because separation can feel isolating.
Because being heard can be healing.
Because stories soften shame.
Because sometimes, you just need a space where you can exhale.
Whether you are going through it, supporting someone who is, or reflecting on your own journey — this evening is an invitation to pause, connect, and remember that you are not alone.
Date: March 15, 2026
Location: Atta Galatta, Bangalore
Duration: 2 hours

name: Plurio Presents: Quizapiens at Atta Galatta - Curiosity. Conversations. Community
description:
Plurio is back with Quizapiens, an engaging, laughter-filled afternoon that brings together movies, music, monuments, and memories in one vibrant gathering. Designed as an intimate, curiosity-led experience, the event blends friendly competition with creative play and thoughtful reflection.
 
Date & Time: 2:00 PM – 5:00 PM
Seats: Limited to 15 participants
 
The afternoon begins with The Big Quiz Hour (2:00 – 3:00 PM), featuring an exciting round of trivia covering movies, music, Indian monuments, and a few delightful surprises. Whether you're a cinema buff, a history enthusiast, or simply someone who enjoys fun facts, the quiz promises spirited participation and plenty of “I knew that!” moments.
After a short refreshment break, participants will dive into Dumb Charades & Pictionary (3:15 – 4:00 PM) — classic crowd favourites that invite dramatic flair, creative clues, wild guesses, and lots of laughter.
The final segment, Postcard Writing (4:15 – 5:00 PM), offers a quieter, reflective close to the day. Attendees will have the opportunity to write to a friend, their future self, or someone they’ve been meaning to reconnect with. Postcards will be provided.
 
Prizes will be awarded to:

The Quiz Winner
The Dumb Charades Champions
The Pictionary Winner

The event concludes with an open Feedback Circle, encouraging shared reflections and community dialogue.
Plurio aims to create meaningful offline spaces for shared joy and conversation. With only 15 seats available, Quizapiens is intentionally designed as a cozy, interactive gathering.
For those who love learning random facts, laughing loudly, and putting pen to paper, this promises to be an afternoon well spent.

name: Postcards To Strangers - Postcard Making Workshop
description:
Have you ever received a postcard from someone you don’t know? That little card, carrying words that somehow feel bigger than the space they hold. 
At Postcards to Strangers, you will create your own hand-made postcard, using colors, stickers, and art supplies provided. You’ll write a message of kindness — something you wish you had heard, or a note you want to share with someone else. Then comes the magic: you’ll exchange your postcard with another participant, and take home one in return. 
This is a space for reflection, creativity, and gentle connection. No artistic skills are needed — just curiosity, an open heart, and the joy of giving and receiving.

name: A STORY WALK through INDIRANAGAR - History Walk
description:
On a Saturday morning , Walk through the lanes of INDIRA NAGAR with Vikram Sridhar discovering the lanes through the Folklore , Heritage and Ecology of the area . The walk will begin and culminate at ATTA GALATTA . The tales will revolve around historical , mythical and personal narratives weaved as we discover the area through the walk .
 Date : 21st March ( Saturday)   
 Time : 7 AM
 Starting Point : Atta Galatta
Registration : 349/ per person 
Ticket:https://in.bookmyshow.com/plays/a-story-walk-through-indiranagar-bangalore/ET00387939 
(or) 
Details and GPAY : 9945799224

name: Tulika Day!- Celebrating 30 Years of Tulika - Children’s Festival
description:
Calling all children! The magic of Tulika books, the much-loved children`s publisher, is being brought to Atta Galatta to celebrate 30 years of Tulika. Join us for a day of interactive book sessions, storytelling, and magical performances!
Register now, don’t miss this day of fun and frolic!
Please Note:
1) Only children need tickets, two parents can accompany each child.
2) Tickets to individual events are not available. Only day passes and half day passes are available.
3) You can access as many or as few of the sessions as you want with the day pass and the half day pass gives you access to the morning and evening events.
 
SCHEDULE:
10:00 AM-Stories with Vikram Uncle
Hehe! Haha! Spend your morning laughing with Vikram uncle as he regales with Tulika tails… erm we mean tales! Giggle, guffaw, grimace and sing along to storyteller Vikram Sridhar.
11:00 AM- What’s hiding in the dark? with Sahitya Rani 
Kishore is away with his father out in the open sea for the first time. He is afraid of the crashing waves, the chaos on deck and most importantly - he is afraid of the dark. 
Are you afraid of the dark? What do you think is hiding there?
12:00 PM-Gajapati Kulapati Korrr with Ashok Rajagopalan
Aaaachooo! Gajapati Kulapati catches a big big cold. Gurrburrroooom! Oh no, Gajapati Kulapati has tummy trouble. Kalicha Kulicha! Gajapati Kulapati dances in muddy puddles. 
And now, poor Gajapati Kulapati has had a bad dream. He’s woken up the whole village with his loud cries and the villagers decide to sing him back to sleep. 

1:00 PM-Lunch Break
2:00 PM-Explore the Night Sky with Ananya Srinivasan
What do you know about the constellations that float above our heads every night? Learn about the night sky and do an activity with illustrator Ananya Srinivasan.
3:00 PM- Jumbo Stories! with Devika Cariapa
What happens when a group of Japanese school children write to ask India’s Prime Minister for a real, live elephant? Find out with author Devika Cariapa as she tells the true story of India’s JUMBO gifts to the world. You may even want to send off a letter of your own!

4:00PM- Fun with Puppets and Tulika Animals by Santosh Kumar

Can you bark like a dog? Can you squeak like a mouse? Santosh Kumar and his trunk full of talking puppets are here to tell us stories that will make your tummy ache from laughing! Let’s see what those puppets are up to!
 
Each session will have a 15 minute gap for refreshments, bio breaks etc.
 
What's included

Day passes include access to all events


Half day passes access 3 events

name: Food in literature - Book club
description:
Our emotions and memories are intrinsically related to the food we eat and the genre of food literature combines them in a way that helps us connect not only to our past and our present, to  different cultures, and communities, values and rituals, events both life-changing and mundane but to each other as well. 
So, if viewing the world through the lens of food and literature interests you, then join us for a  dialogue on “Food in Literature” and to celebrate those stories and their diversity. Each month with  Prachi Grover, we read our way through several powerful (and delicious) narratives.
March Read: Stories from a Kargili Kitchen by Yash Saxena
We assure you that you will enjoy every single bite!
The session is free but registration is a must as we have limited spots available. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a community for people who love to read and eat!
When: Saturday, 21st March, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Registration is mandatory (link in bio)
Copies of the book are available at the store.

name: The Bougainvillea Circle - Connection Circle
description:
"Sawubona" a Zulu Greeting meaning, "We See You". At Bougainvillea Circles you are offered a space to be heard, seen, welcomed as you are. A non-judgmental, safe, trusted space where you are free to be all that you want to be. Nothing More, Nothing less. No Restrictions, no judgements, just an intimate group of people looking for a space to be real. A space where we`re all less alone for a little while. Where we are seen, how we want to be seen. Conversations, Connections, Community of Compassion. All that makes the world go round, after Love of course!
Remember - Sawubona: We See You.

name: A STORY WALK through DOMLUR  - Story Walk by Vikram Sridhar
description:
On a Sunday morning , Walk through the lanes of DOMLUR , from lores dating back in time from the Madras Presidency , Mysore State to the current IT , Banking and Retail sectors with Vikram Sridhar discovering the lanes through the Folklore , Heritage and Ecology of the rich area which is identified as a village. The walk will begin from Bhandutvas , a theatre collective , and culminate at the Bangalore International Centre . The tales will revolve around historical , mythical and personal narratives weaved as we discover the area through the walk .
The Story walk is supported by Atta Galatta
 *Date* : 22nd March  (Sunday)  2026
 *Time* : 7 AM
 *Starting Point* : Bhandutvas , Domlur
Registration : 349/ per person 
Ticket Link : https://in.bookmyshow.com/plays/a-story-walk-through-domlur-bangalore/ET00465609
(or) 
Details and GPAY : 9945799224

name: Once Upon A Brunch - Sunday morning brunch and stories
description:
Late weekend morning , brunch , stories like the ones from the olden times, meeting new people. What if they all come together ?
Weekend and late morning Brunch is an emotion . On the other hand, stories have always been an emotion too . What's common between the two is the rich heritage of food and stories. What if they come together ? .
Listen to these stories of the food as you enjoy your weekend Brunch set at a cozy location that has its own stories . A beautiful day starts with a lovely breakfast . And a morning with breakfast and stories . Every food we eat has a story to share .. Idli , Vada , bread , Paratha etc . Every breakfast comes after a long break and is eager to be eaten . A simple Weekend morning Brunch put together by Atta Galatta
Storytelling around the food lores by Vikram Sridhar

name: Listening with Colours - An Art Journaling Workshop
description:
When was the last time you truly listened to yourself? Not through a to-do list, not through your phone, but quietly… with colours in your hands. Maybe even crayons.
This Sunday, join Yukti Grover and Team Pause and Breathe for a slow, reflective space where you can pause for a moment and simply notice what’s within. In a room of strangers, or maybe even a few familiar faces, we’ll take a pause and breathe together, set perfection aside, and let colours help us listen to ourselves and others better.
No pressure to be “artistic,” and no right or wrong way to show up. Come curious. Come tired. Come exactly as you are.
Date: Sunday, 22nd March
Time: 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM

name: Spark Squad Huddle Playdate - Nature-Led Play – The ISSM Way
description:
Playissm invites children and families to a joyful and meaningful play experience through Spark Squad Huddle, a thoughtfully designed playdate rooted in the ISSM approach to learning and development.
This special session explores Nature-Led Play, where children engage with natural materials, sensory elements, and open-ended activities that encourage curiosity, creativity, and exploration.
Guided by the principles of ISSM - Immersive, Self-Directed, Synergetic, and Mindful play, the session creates a nurturing environment where children can build, imagine, connect, and discover at their own pace.
 
Children will engage in activities such as:
Nature-inspired sensory exploration
Open-ended creative play with natural materials
Collaborative building and imaginative play
Calm and mindful moments in nature
 
Spark Squad Huddle is designed to be screen-free, sensory-rich, and child-led, allowing children to experience the joy of play while developing confidence, creativity, and social connection.

Theme: Nature-Led Play – The ISSM Way (All Materials Included)
Date: Sunday, 22nd March 2026
Time: 1:30 PM
Age Group: 2 – 10 years
Fee: ₹1200 per child
Designed & Facilitated by:
Neha Joshi
Founder – Playissm
MSc Child Development
This event is a collaboration between Playissm and Atta Galatta, bringing together play, learning, and community in a warm and welcoming environment for families.

name: Humzubaan: Hindi & Urdu Poetry Meet - Poetry Open Mic
description:
Humzubaan, conceived by Siddharth Surana is an effort to bring together Hindi/Urdu literature enthusiasts together to celebrate the beauty of poetry. The next meet up is happening on Friday, 27th March, 6 PM at Atta Galatta.
Recite your own or your favourite author’s poems or simply soak in the magic of poetry. Click Here to register as audience or performer. Performance Submissions are open till 22nd March.
We will inform you if your poem is selected for the meet. Even if your poem is not selected, you’re most welcome to attend as an audience.
Please note:
1. The poems can be your own original work or of another author. You need to mention clearly and send in your entry in the above-mentioned form.
2. Poems/songs should be in Hindi/Urdu only.
3. If you’re planning to perform with music/props, please mention in the form. You would need to arrange for the instruments and set-up at the venue.
 
About the host:
Siddharth is founder of AgriCentral, a digital platform serving 11 million farmers. He writes in Hindi, Urdu and English-mostly poems, short stories and essays but with long breaks. At least three books are jostling to come out of his mind on paper, for two decades, but none has seen the light of the day, as yet. Hopefully, it will change soon. By the way. he is also an avid trekker and a national-level gold medallist archer.
Age: 4 and above

name: Brand Basics for First-Time Entrepreneurs - Entrepreneurship Workshop
description:
For the first time, First Brick Collective is bringing together two different but equally important pieces of the entrepreneurial journey: shaping your idea and understanding how you communicate it.
Sudhir Kiran will be walking participants through the basics of building a brand and finding the right words to explain what they’re building, and Shravan will help them understand their natural communication style and how it influences the way they lead, pitch, and build relationships.
Two founders. Two frameworks. One empowering afternoon.
Limited seats. Link in bio.
 
Age: 18 and above
performer: by Sudhir Kiran & First Brick Collective

name: DIY Leather Journal Workshop - Workshop
description:
In a world that rarely pauses, creating something by hand can feel deeply grounding.
HARTS Lifestyle & Gifting invites you to a special DIY Leather Journal Crafting Workshop at Atta Galatta, where participants will experience the joy of slow, hands-on creation.
More than a craft activity, the workshop is a quiet invitation to step away from screens, work with your hands, and create an object that becomes a personal space for thoughts, ideas, and stories.
This immersive workshop is designed for anyone who values creativity, reflection, and the beauty of making something personal.
During the session, participants will assemble their own leather journal from scratch — choosing their preferred journal size and leather cover, learning the basics of journal construction, and personalising the final piece with their name or initials.
Each participant will leave with a handcrafted, personalised leather journal — created by them, for them.
Hosted at the warm and creative space of Atta Galatta, the workshop promises a relaxed environment for makers, writers, thinkers, and curious beginners alike.

All materials included. No prior experience required.
Age: 7-18 yrs

name: Unspoken: A Language Of The Eyes - An Evening Of Expression
description:
An intimate 60-minute interactive experience exploring everyday human emotions through the expressive language of classical art form.
Designed for café spaces, this session blends classical Abhinaya with modern emotional storytelling in a conversational, accessible format.
Open to all — no dance background required.
A curated cultural evening that brings community and expression in a meaningful way.

name: Bangalore Storytelling Society - A story telling meetup
description:
Bangalore Storytelling Society is hosting a family extravaganza for World Storytelling Day. Just like stories are for everyone, this curated performance is designed for an interactive, wholesome experience for friends and families. Our handpicked tellers have poignant, reflective, energetic,  quirky bags of stories that blend and blur the boundaries of age.
Join us on March 29th, 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM at Atta Galatta. Book your tickets as we turn ‘Light into Dark” , one story at a time.

name: Brachio - Interactive Dramatic Reading
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for an interactive dramatic reading by Venkataraghavan, where the author brings his story to life by performing it as a lively play.
 
About the Book:
Brachio lives by the beach, loves helping people and is very, very tall.
But what will he do when the village-folk no longer need him?
 
As he embarks on a remarkable journey, Brachio becomes a star, gets hit by
fluffy balls and makes a great escape!
Will Brachio ever discover what it truly means to be tall?
 
About the Author:
Venkataraghavan is so tall that he can touch most ceilings. He was the first person to receive the NLF Fellowship for Children's Book Creators.

name: Music of Mystics - An Evening of Sufi, Qawwali & Ghazals
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for Music of Mystics, an evening dedicated to Sufi, Qawwali and Ghazals, led by Hindustani vocalist Ustad Ashraf Hydroz, Kerala Sangeetha Nataka Akademi Awardee.
Rooted in the Sufi understanding of Ishq — a love that goes beyond the self — and Noor, the light believed to reside within every heart, the concert reflects on longing, surrender and remembrance through music. The repertoire moves between traditional Qawwalis, reflective Sufi compositions and timeless Ghazals, allowing poetry and melody to unfold at their own pace.
Accompanied by Kabeer (Harmonium), Ullas (SaiBanjo), Sirajudheen  (Dholak) and Dileep Vikram  (Tabla), the ensemble brings together classical structure and emotional depth in an intimate live setting
 
March 29 | 6 PM
Age: All
Atta Galatta, Indiranagar, Bengaluru

name: My Home is Dissent - Book Launch & Poetry Workshop
description:
My Home is Dissent is a luminous poetry collection by Pooja Ugrani that constructs verse as one might build a home—brick by tender brick, with the mortar of memory, desire, and defiance. Published by Poetrywala, an imprint of Paperwall Publishing, this collection moves fluidly between the everyday and the elemental, where the domestic becomes a site of quiet resistance and reimagining. Ugrani's dissent hums through acts of nurture and refusal, through the courage to remain soft in a world that demands armour. Her poems measure the dimensions of love, labour, and loss through the delicate instruments of memory, architecture, and motherhood. Formally supple and sensorial, cerebral and sensuous, her work folds the architectural and the emotional into one continuum. This world of words writes of labour, love, and dissent as acts of design—as deliberate gestures that hold the self together. Praised by acclaimed authors including Sampurna Chattarji, Maithreyi Karnoor, and poet Srividya Sivakumar, this collection participates physically in the making of poetry, not only through language but also through the palpability of the body's experiences. Here is a poet testing her wings, where dissension may well be a heartbeat away from dissection. It is currently out on Amazon for sale: https://amzn.in/d/0gY3DXTf
 
About the Poet: Pooja Ugrani, architect, teacher and mother, lives her life in two cities, Bombay and Bangalore.
Her poems are included in the anthologies The Shape of a Poem - The Red River Book of Contemporary Erotic Poetry, The Kali project anthology - Invoking the Goddess within/ Indian Women's Voices by Indie Blue Publishing LLC, the Yearbook of Indian Poetry 2022 and Po'try, an anthology of Bangalore poets. Her poem, Gundalamma Palem was shortlisted as one of the forty poems out of over thousand international entries for the Alpine Fellowship 2023. Her poetry has been published in The Punch Magazine, Cafe Dissensus Everyday, Mom Egg Review (New York), Hākārā, Voice and Verse Poetry Magazine (Hong Kong), The Tiger Moth Review (Singapore), The Alipore Post, The Woman Inc - TWI Poetry, and the RIC Journal (Jaipur-Paris). Ugrani was invited to read at the Bangalore Poetry Festival and by the Champaca bookstore at the Bangalore International Center on the occasion of International Womens' Day. She was a featured poet at Tuesdays with the Bard at Urban Solace, Ulsoor, in December 2019. While she is relaxing, Pooja Ugrani explores origami with children and adults, sings Bollywood songs and dreams of a trek in the Himalayas.
Age: 15 and above

name: Muqadamma – The Trial - A Poetic Musical courtroom drama of Three Witnesses
description:
Muqadamma unfolds through three witnesses —
one who never spoke,
one who chose silence,
and one who was silenced… until even the ruins began to speak.
Created and performed by Ruha Latif — a practicing civil litigation advocate, a woman’s rights activist and a speaker on gender discrimination and Shariah rights of Women, the author of “Rooh Dekhi Hai Kabhi (Part 1 & 2)”, Ballads from a Mother’s Heart & Conversations, and a performing artist who bridges courtroom and poetry.
Accompanied by live violin and mandolin by Utsav Biswas , he has trained in both Indian and western classical music. He loves to explore how emotions and music intertwine. 
This gathering is more like witnessing a courtroom trial.

Age: all

name: Rainbow Journal - Meet-up and  Workshop
description:
Rainbow Journaling is a creative, reflective workshop that invites you to explore your thoughts, stories, and emotions through colour and words.
Using guided prompts, colours, and simple journaling exercises, this session creates a relaxed space to slow down, reflect, and put your thoughts onto the page. Think of it as a pause from the noise of everyday life — a chance to sit with your ideas, express yourself freely, and see what emerges.
This is not about perfect writing or polished storytelling. It’s about curiosity, creativity, and letting the page hold whatever you feel like expressing.
✨ Guided prompts to spark reflection
✨ Creative journaling using colour and words
✨ A calm, welcoming space to write and think
✨ A chance to explore the many shades of your story
Come as you are... all you need is an open mind and a willingness to write.
Age: 18 and above

name: The Adventures of Rifa - Book Reading and Signing
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for the book launch of The Adventures of Rifa by Samina Mahmood, a charming children’s book that brings alive the joys, mischief and wonder of growing up in a hill station in the 1950s.
Through the eyes of Rifa—curious, courageous and delightfully mischievous—the book follows a series of small but memorable adventures shared with her brother, cousins and friends. Set against the backdrop of sun-dappled days and rainy afternoons, these stories capture the innocence of childhood while gently reflecting on the lessons, values and friendships that shape us as we grow.
Join us for a warm and engaging session with the author as she introduces young readers to Rifa’s world and shares the memories and inspirations behind the book.

name: Keeping A Sketchbook Diary - an introduction to gouache techniques
description:
Have you ever wanted to capture a moment — not just with a photo, but with your hands? This workshop is an invitation to slow down, look closely, and make something that is entirely yours.
In two hours, you'll move through three acts: first, a set of guided observation exercises designed to train your eye — noticing colour, negative space, and the shapes hiding in plain sight. Then, a reflection practice using poetry, short passages, and journal prompts to find the words for what you've seen. Finally, integration — weaving your sketches and writing into a single page, and learning how to carry this practice home.
No experience needed. Gouache is a forgiving, vibrant medium — bright payoff, slow pace, good company.
All Materials are included.
Age: 16+

name: THE COMMUNITY ART CIRCLE - Wonders Of Team Work In Art And Story Making
description:
A fun take on art with a community team spirit.
Seated in a circle layout, the imagination comes to life with basic art skills, inspiring stories and creative expressions.
Take home a creation inspiring stories, memories and a lot of interpretations!
Art. Story. Repeat!

For Aditi, creativity is a loop of endless possibility: art sparks a character, and story breathes life through imagination.
After beginning her career as a UX/UI Design professional in Bengaluru and pursuing it for more than a decade; she has been pursuing the joy of storytelling through her venture, Band of Beads. Narratives are woven through beading, fabrics, and puppetry - crafting tiny worlds with big hearts.

name: THE ROAR AND THE SQUEAK! - Puppet Show, Art Session And Some Fun-Time
description:
The mighty lion and the tiny mouse return in a charming puppet show filled with sing-along songs and storytelling through handmade puppets. Children will then be guided through an art activity to create an animated lion – the imaginative storytelling version of the character on paper!
A perfect blend of art, imagination, and storytelling: come and create your own jungle tale!

For Aditi, creativity is a loop of endless possibility: art sparks a character, and story breathes life through imagination.
After beginning her career as a UX/UI Design professional in Bengaluru and pursuing it for more than a decade; she has been pursuing the joy of storytelling through her venture, Band of Beads. Narratives are woven through beading, fabrics, and puppetry - crafting tiny worlds with big hearts.
Art. Story. Repeat!
